7

years of Studio
How About That!

An ode to the worlds we've called home
THE CORRIDOR
Seven Years · Seven Worlds
THE CONFESSION · 01

Here is what nobody tells you about spending seven years running a creative studio.

THE THESIS · 02

You don't build a style.
You absorb worlds.

THE MEAL · 03

Art Deco. Tropicália. Soviet propaganda. Bollywood posters. Japanese city pop. The sleeve of a Factory record. You absorb all of it — slowly, deliberately, with full attention — and it changes the composition of what you make.

ANTROPOFAGIA · 04

The Tropicalists had a name for this. Antropofagia. Cultural cannibalism. You devour what you love, and you become something that could not have existed without it.

WORLD 01 · OPEN
A world is open. Enter here.
SIX ACTS OF LOVE · 05

Six years of that. Six worlds. Six acts of love toward the things that shaped us.

WORLD 02 · OPEN
A world is open. Enter here.
THE REACTION · 06

Every day for seven years, a specific group of people in a specific city has been saying the same three words. Not as a question. As a reaction. The thing you say when you walk into a room and it stops you.

How About That!

WORLD 03
OCT 2026
THE THREAD · 07

They build. Each world references the next — a detail that only makes sense in retrospect. A thread runs through all six.

THE SEVENTH · 08

And then there is the seventh. The seventh world is the one that happens after you've absorbed everything — the world you make when you stop looking outward and look at what you've become.

WORLD 07 · THE UNVEILING

Seven years. Seven worlds.
The last one: ours.

Unveiled in person — 31 August, at the studio, Bangalore. An RSVP evening; the door opens for the first time that night.
